assertion.title: GP operator pruning: core 13-op subset improves fallback discovery 9/14→10/14
assertion.statement: Restricting GP random tree generation and point mutation to 13 core operators (4 binary + 9 unary) while keeping all 72 operators for templates, evaluation, and simplification improves GP fallback discovery rate from 9/14 to 10/14 exact matches and raises mean R² from 0.9911 to 0.9940.

details.hypothesis: The 72-operator search space degrades GP performance when templates fail to match. Restricting random tree generation and mutation to a core subset of fundamental operators (add/sub/mul/div + neg/square/sqrt/sin/cos/exp/log/inv/negexp) should improve discovery by reducing the search space while keeping specialized operators accessible via templates and simplification rules.
changes: Added GP_BINARY_OPS = ['add', 'sub', 'mul', 'div'] — core binary ops for GP search
Modified random_tree() to use GP_BINARY_OPS/GP_UNARY_OPS instead of full BINARY_OPS/UNARY_OPS (removed ternary/quaternary from random generation)
Modified point_mutate() to use core ops for binary/unary mutation (ternary/quaternary from templates preserved)

gp_fallback_comparison.test_conditions: Template fast-path disabled, 30s budget per equation, single restart

pruned_wins.eq39: not exact (25 nodes) → EXACT (7 nodes, radioactive decay)

full_wins.eq34: EXACT → R²=0.9898 (Gaussian: had gaussian op in search)
full_wins.eq23: EXACT → R²=0.9993 (Lorentz: had mullorentz op in search)

results.key_insight: Specialized compound operators (mullorentz, gaussian, boltzmann3, etc.) are valuable as template building blocks and simplification targets, but they dilute the GP search space when used in random tree generation. A focused core set of fundamental mathematical operations allows the GP to explore more efficiently. The 2 equations where full ops win (eq23, eq34) happen to have their exact compound operator randomly selected — a lucky hit rather than systematic discovery.
